_CLAUDE_READ_THIS_FIRST:
MANDATORY — READ BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE.

This is GSI_Session.json v2.0 — the SESSION MANIFEST for the Global Stock Intelligence Dashboard.
It tracks session history, file versions, open items, and per-file next steps.
Stable reference (file structure, rules, architecture, API behavior) lives in CLAUDE.md in the repo root.

ON SESSION START:
 1. Read this manifest fully — confirm session number, current version, open items.
 2. Read CLAUDE.md if you do not have it in context (repo root or ask user to paste).
 3. Run python regression.py — confirm 189/189 PASS before any new work.
 4. Follow session_start_checklist for which Gists/files to fetch.

DURING SESSION:
 5. Log every patch, fix, decision as an activity entry in sessions[N].activities as it happens.

ON SESSION END — NON-NEGOTIABLE:
 6. Update sessions[N].activities with all activities.
 7. Update file_state for every file touched (version, lines, last_confirmed).
 8. Update file_registry[file].change_history and next_steps for touched files.
 9. Update open_items — close completed, add new ones.
10. Update regression.last_run and last_result.
11. If architecture changed — update CLAUDE.md too.
12. Deliver updated GSI_Session.json as downloadable file.
13. Remind user to push CLAUDE.md to repo and update Gist: https://gist.github.com/Tarun19k/7c894c02dad4e76fe7c404bf963baeab

Resume prompt: 'I am working on the Global Stock Intelligence Dashboard. Here is the session manifest GSI_Session.json — read it fully and confirm our state before we proceed.'

meta.protocol: This manifest MUST be updated after every patch, fix, rebuild, or architectural change to the project. Updating the manifest is itself a required activity — marked IMPORTANT — and must occur before or towards the end of every session. At the start of a new session, provide the Gist URL and say: 'Here is the session manifest — continue from where we left off.' Claude will read it and resume without re-reading every file from GitHub.
meta.version_convention: Increment minor version (v5.X) for any patch or fix. Increment major version (vX.0) for structural refactors. Always add entry to version.py VERSION_LOG before shipping.
meta.deployment_target: Both — Streamlit Community Cloud AND local dev
meta.run_regression: python regression.py # must be run from project root

github_constraints.root_files: Readable via blob URLs — Claude can fetch these without upload
github_constraints.pages_subdir: BLOCKED by robots.txt — use Pages Gist instead
github_constraints.rate_limiting: GitHub rate-limits Claude fetches. If a fetch fails, use the Gist URL instead.
github_constraints.raw_urls_blocked: raw.githubusercontent.com URLs are blocked unless they appear in search results first. Use github.com/blob/ URLs instead.
github_constraints.html_truncation_warning: GitHub blob page HTML includes full file content embedded in the page. Large files (>400 lines) served alongside other files in a single Gist fetch will truncate. ALWAYS use dedicated single-file Gists for large files. See gist_truncation_policy.

meta.repo_relationship: TWO REPOS EXIST — do not confuse them. Repo 1 (global-gsi-intelligence): the original monolith — 2451 lines in one file. REFERENCE ONLY — no active development. Repo 2 (stock-intelligence-test-1-vs): the modular refactor — ALL active work happens here. Every fix, patch, and rebuild in this manifest applies to Repo 2 only.
meta.claude_md: CLAUDE.md in repo root — stable cold-start reference. Contains: file structure, run commands, do-not-undo rules, architecture decisions, environment, API behavior, scoring model, regression table. Update CLAUDE.md when architecture changes; update this manifest every session.
meta.manifest_note: v2.0: stable reference sections moved to CLAUDE.md. This manifest is now session-state only: activities, file versions, Gist URLs, open items, file registry, session protocol.

gist_index.description: Master index of all Gist URLs used in this project. ALWAYS add new files here when creating or moving to a Gist. This is the single source of truth for code access.

truncation_policy.rule: Any file over ~300 lines must have its own dedicated single-file Gist. Never share a Gist page with another large file.
truncation_policy.reason: Claude's web_fetch tool returns a fixed-size HTML response. When a Gist page contains multiple large files, the HTML is truncated mid-page and files appearing later in the page are silently missing. This was discovered in session 002 when GSI_session.json (~860 lines) caused version.py, tickers.json, regression.py, and market_data.py to be invisible in the root Gist fetch.
truncation_policy.lesson: If Claude reports a file as 'missing' from a Gist, the real cause is almost always truncation — not absence. Always verify by fetching the dedicated Gist URL for that file.

size_thresholds.borderline: 200-400 lines — use judgement based on other files in same Gist

GSI_session.reason: Dedicated — too large to share (860+ lines, grows each session)

tickers.reason: Dedicated — too large to share (655 lines of JSON)

pages.reason: Multi-file Gist safe — total lines spread across 4 files, no single file dominates the page
pages.update_frequency: When any pages/ file is patched

root_files.reason: Multi-file Gist — safe as long as no single file exceeds 400 lines
root_files.warning: version.py, tickers.json, regression.py, market_data.py were previously in this Gist but caused truncation. They now have dedicated Gists or GitHub blob URLs.
root_files.update_frequency: When any root file is patched

add_new_file_procedure.step_2: If > 400 lines — create a dedicated single-file Gist
add_new_file_procedure.step_3: If < 200 lines — can be added to an existing multi-file Gist IF total page size stays reasonable
add_new_file_procedure.step_4: Add the new Gist URL to gist_index.gists in this manifest
add_new_file_procedure.step_6: Update manifest and push updated GSI_Session.json to its Gist

activities.label: Gist truncation problem discovered and root-caused
activities.detail: When Claude fetched the root Gist (ae1c6f3cda019baa8c831cad38da2d71) containing 8 files including GSI_session.json (~860 lines), the HTML response was truncated. Root cause: Gist HTML pages embed full file content — large files consume the fixed fetch response budget, leaving no room for subsequent files in the page.
activities.lesson: Never put files >400 lines in a shared Gist. Create dedicated single-file Gists for large files. When Claude reports a file as missing from a Gist, always suspect truncation first.

open_items.label: Portfolio Allocator — Mean-CVaR optimisation tab in group overview

detail.what: A new 'Portfolio Allocator' tab added to the group overview page (State 3 — group selected, no stock). Given a list of stocks in the group, it computes the optimal allocation using Mean-CVaR portfolio optimisation and presents it as: amount slider + risk dial (Conservative/Balanced/Aggressive) + allocation bar chart + rupee amounts per stock + efficient frontier chart + plain-English explanation.

algorithm.reference_paper: Rockafellar R.T. & Uryasev S. (2000). Optimization of conditional value-at-risk. Journal of Risk, 2, 21-42.
algorithm.why_not_nvidia_directly: NVIDIA blueprint requires GPU hardware (cuOpt solver, CUDA container). Not viable for Streamlit Community Cloud deployment. Same maths implementable in pure Python — at retail scale (15-50 stocks), scipy/cvxpy solves in <200ms on CPU.
steps: 1. Fetch 90-day daily close prices for all stocks in the group via get_price_data()
2. Compute daily log returns: log(P_t / P_{t-1})
3. Generate 10,000 return scenarios by bootstrapping historical returns (same method as compute_forecast in indicators.py — preserves fat tails, no normal distribution assumed)
4. Formulate Mean-CVaR linear programme: maximise E[return] - lambda * CVaR(alpha=0.95)
where lambda = risk_aversion param (Conservative=3.0, Balanced=1.0, Aggressive=0.3)
5. Apply constraints: sum(weights)=1, 0<=w_i<=0.35 (max 35% per stock), min 5 stocks held
6. Solve using cvxpy (open source, CPU, pip installable)
7. Return weights, expected annual return, CVaR, Sharpe ratio, efficient frontier points
algorithm.cvar_explained: CVaR at 95% confidence = average loss in the worst 5% of scenarios. Better than standard deviation because it captures tail risk and asymmetric distributions. Used in Basel III bank risk rules. Conservative target: CVaR < 4%, Balanced: < 7%, Aggressive: < 11%.
algorithm.efficient_frontier: Solve the optimisation at 10-15 different lambda values to trace the full frontier. Plot risk (CVaR) on x-axis, expected return on y-axis. User's selected portfolio shown as highlighted dot on the curve.

python_libraries.cvxpy: pip install cvxpy — convex optimisation solver, open source, CPU only
python_libraries.PyPortfolioOpt: pip install PyPortfolioOpt — optional, has EfficientCVaR class that wraps same maths

detail.estimated_scope: v5.23: portfolio.py + Allocator tab + Layer 4 regime conflict check (~1 session). v5.24: Backtest engine + stability score + CVaR calibration tracker (~1 session).
detail.prerequisite: cvxpy must be installable in deployment environment (Streamlit Cloud supports it)

validation_framework.philosophy: The model cannot be 100% fail-proof — no optimiser can account for events that haven't happened yet (earnings, macro, black swans). The goal is to know WHEN the model is likely to be wrong and surface that clearly. Fail loudly and visibly, not silently.

layer_1_backtesting.what: Run optimiser at 50+ historical entry points across different market regimes
layer_1_backtesting.metric: Average return at 21d and 63d horizons vs equal-weight benchmark. % of periods model beat benchmark.
layer_1_backtesting.shown_in_ui: Backtest summary card on Allocator tab: 'Model beat equal-weight in 68% of past 21-day periods'

layer_2_stability.what: Perturbation test — small input changes should not cause large allocation flips
layer_2_stability.method: Re-run optimiser 20 times with ±5% random noise on returns. Measure std dev of each weight.
layer_2_stability.threshold: Flag unstable if any stock weight std dev > 15% across perturbations
layer_2_stability.shown_in_ui: Stability badge: STABLE / MODERATE / UNSTABLE shown next to allocation

layer_3_cvar_calibration.what: Track whether predicted CVaR matches actual observed worst-day losses
layer_3_cvar_calibration.method: Store predicted CVaR in forecast_history.json alongside portfolio forecasts. After 21d, resolve: was actual worst-day loss within CVaR bound?
layer_3_cvar_calibration.metric: CVaR hit rate per group — 'Conservative CVaR was accurate 82% of the time for Indian IT'
layer_3_cvar_calibration.shown_in_ui: Same calibration tracker as forecast accuracy on Week Summary page, but for portfolio CVaR
layer_3_cvar_calibration.integration: Extends existing get_weekly_accuracy_report() — same schema, new entry_type='portfolio'

layer_4_regime_conflict.what: Cross-check optimiser output against Weinstein/Elder signals before showing to user
rules: If optimiser weight >= 10% AND stock verdict == AVOID → show red conflict banner
If >30% of portfolio in Stage-4 stocks → show 'Regime warning: trend cycle does not support this allocation'
If Elder weekly tide is bearish for >50% of allocated stocks → suppress BUY language in verdict
layer_4_regime_conflict.shown_in_ui: Same conflict banner pattern as unified_verdict in indicators.py — shown BEFORE allocation, not after
layer_4_regime_conflict.override: User can proceed with acknowledgement checkbox — not silently overridden

validation_framework.disclaimer_required_in_ui: Past returns used to model future risk. Does not account for earnings, macro events, or regulatory changes. Not financial advice. The Insights tab signals should be read alongside this allocation.
what_we_cannot_prevent: Black swan events (COVID, geopolitical shock, regulatory ban)
Earnings surprises that gap a stock 20%+ overnight
Sector bans or regulatory action (e.g. Chinese tech crackdown)
Systemic correlation breakdown — all stocks fall together in a crisis
validation_framework.how_to_communicate_uncertainty: Show a confidence band on expected return, not a single number. Conservative portfolio: 'Expected +14% ± 6% at 21 days'. Aggressive portfolio: 'Expected +24% ± 15% at 21 days'. Wider band = less certainty = user should size position smaller.

critical.name: The Panic Seller
critical.scenario: Market drops 8% in a day. RSI fires STRONG BUY on everything.
critical.failure: User buys into a falling market on a signal that fires precisely because market is crashing.
critical.fix: VIX > 25 or daily drop > 3% → STRESS MODE banner. Widen CVaR to aggressive-only. No allocation shown until regime stabilises.

critical.name: COVID-style crash
critical.scenario: All stocks drop 30-40% in 3 weeks. All correlations go to 1.
critical.failure: Diversification fails. CVaR estimates from before crash are completely wrong. Model shows confident allocation in worst possible conditions.
critical.fix: Correlation matrix check: if 30-day rolling correlation across group > 0.85, flag CRISIS REGIME. CVaR marked UNRELIABLE. Show wider confidence band.

high.name: Debt-Funded Investor
high.scenario: User invests borrowed money. Conservative CVaR -4% = -20% on 5x leverage.
high.failure: CVaR applies to portfolio value not user equity. Leverage multiplies losses.
high.fix: Leverage disclaimer on aggressive profile. Cannot detect — only warn clearly.

high.name: yfinance bad data
high.scenario: Zero-volume days, stale prices, market halt artifacts in input data.
high.failure: Silent data corruption produces confident-looking but wrong scenarios.
high.fix: Data quality validator in portfolio.py: detect zero-volume days and >15% single-day moves before optimisation.

high.name: Corporate action artifacts
high.scenario: Stock split causes -80% then +400% in log returns. Bootstrap samples this.
high.failure: Single bad data point corrupts all 10,000 bootstrap scenarios.
high.fix: Winsorize returns at 99th percentile in portfolio.py AND in compute_forecast.

high.name: Stationarity failure
high.scenario: 90-day lookback spans regime change: 45 days bull, 45 days bear.
high.failure: Expected return is average of two regimes — describes neither. CVaR underestimated.
high.fix: Exponentially weighted bootstrap: recent days sampled more frequently. Same principle as blended drift in compute_forecast.

medium.fix: Future: portfolio health check — enter custom allocation, see CVaR consequences.
Horizon disclaimer on allocator. Min 21-day horizon stated explicitly.
Currency disclaimer per market. Returns in INR labelled clearly.
ADX < 15 for 30+ days → show equal-weight as alternative.
Relative strength vs Nifty 50 shown per group. Cross-group rotation warning.
Minimum 90-day history gate. IPO stocks excluded from optimiser with label.
Document correlation instability as known limitation. Min 5 stocks mitigates.
Min 5% position floor. Post-cost return estimate (deduct 0.25% round-trip).

signal_architecture.description: Layered adjustment pipeline — each layer is small, transparent, independently defensible
pipeline: 5. Macro regime flag: VIX + 10Y yield direction + USD + gold (Risk-On/Off/Transitioning)
6. FII/DII flow bias (net sellers > 5 days → tighten CVaR)
7. Earnings proximity guard (within 14 days → cap at 15% max)
8. News sentiment score (RSS feeds already in GSI → weight adjustment)
9. CVaR optimisation with regime-adjusted risk aversion
11. Minimum 5% position floor + post-cost return estimate
12. Final allocation with confidence band (not point estimate)

free_signals_to_add.FII_DII_flows: NSE/SEBI daily CSV — free, published end of day
free_signals_to_add.News_sentiment: RSS feeds already in GSI — score existing feed

paid_signals_deferred.reason: Priced for institutions managing billions — wrong investment for retail tool
paid_signals_deferred.examples: Satellite imagery (Planet Labs ~$100K/yr), credit card data (Second Measure ~$50K/yr)

honest_scorecard.risk_measurement: 60% — CVaR without Monte Carlo or stress scenario library
honest_scorecard.regime_detection: 45% — VIX+technical vs Hidden Markov Models+macro factors
honest_scorecard.rebalancing: 20% — manual vs automated drift monitoring + tax optimisation
honest_scorecard.transaction_costs: 30% — flat estimate vs full impact model
honest_scorecard.alternative_data: 25% — free proxies vs satellite+credit card+web scraping

institutional_benchmark.key_insight: Institutional frameworks are better at precision. GSI can be better at accessibility and honest communication of uncertainty. A point estimate with no uncertainty range is LESS useful to a retail investor than '+14% ± 8% at 21 days, model unreliable if VIX > 25'. GSI's competitive advantage is honest uncertainty — not false precision.
where_gsi_exceeds_institutional: Plain English explanation of every signal and its limitations
Explicit conflict detection (regime vs momentum — no institution shows this to retail clients)
Failure modes communicated before user acts, not in footnotes
